name: Claudia Sheinbaum
role: President of Mexico — FIFA World Cup 2026 Security Deployment, Apr 22, 2026
quote: We will deploy up to 100,000 security personnel across Guadalajara, Mexico City, and Monterrey for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Guadalajara is a world-class city and Mexico will demonstrate that security and hospitality go hand in hand. The cartel succession crisis in Jalisco will not define our country on the world stage. Following April 20 events at Teotihuacán, Mexico has ordered increased National Guard presence at all major archaeological sites and tourist venues nationwide — additional to the World Cup security framework, not a substitution for it. 50 days to the June 11 opening match.

name: Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes
role: CJNG Founder & Supreme Leader (d. Feb 22, 2026)
quote: El Mencho built the most powerful criminal organization in the Western Hemisphere from a rural Michoacán village. His February 2026 death leaves a power vacuum with no clear successor.

name: Juan Carlos Valencia González
role: CJNG New Leader — Confirmed by El País (Apr 6, 2026) ('O3') — $5M U.S. Bounty
quote: El País (April 6, 2026) confirmed O3 as CJNG's new leader — the most credible international media confirmation of the post-Mencho succession to date. As El Mencho's stepson and commander of Grupo Élite, CJNG's paramilitary unit, O3 commands the cartel's greatest concentrated firepower. Born September 12, 1984 in Santa Ana, California — his U.S. citizenship creates unique legal exposure and complicates bilateral targeting options. Succession field has narrowed significantly: El Doble R arrested, El Tuli killed, El Jardinero primary remaining rival. (Sources: El País, InSight Crime, U.S. State Dept, IBTimes UK)

name: Ricardo Ruiz Velasco
role: CJNG Senior Operational Commander ('El Doble R') — ARRESTED March 11, 2026
quote: Arrested March 11, 2026 in Mexicali, Baja California — charged with homicide, terrorist acts, and armed robbery. El Doble R was widely considered the top succession candidate after El Mencho. His capture eliminates the most formidable internal consolidator from the succession race, narrowing the field to O3 and El Jardinero. (Sources: La Silla Rota, El Financiero)

name: Audias Flores Silva
role: CJNG Senior Regional Commander ('El Jardinero' / The Gardener) — $5M U.S. Bounty Active — AT WAR WITH O3/LOS CUINIS (declared Apr 11, 2026)
quote: Nine days into El Jardinero's declared war on O3 and Los Cuinis (April 11, 2026), the El Jardinero/El Sapo faction has produced escalatory operational activity in its territorial zone: a Zacatecas-Jalisco border police HQ attack (April 12), and the April 18 deployment of 'Monstruos' (improvised armored vehicles) across six municipalities on the Jalisco-Zacatecas border near Colotlán — consistent with military-grade pre-combat positioning. El Jardinero's multi-state plaza network spans Jalisco, Nayarit, Zacatecas, Michoacán, and Guerrero — a broader territorial base than O3's Grupo Élite paramilitary stronghold. Both factions remain in sustained buildup with no direct heartland combat confirmed in the CJNG core Jalisco territory. Status: at large; active leader of the El Jardinero/El Sapo anti-O3 armed faction. $5M U.S. State Department bounty active. (Sources: El Heraldo de México, Borderland Beat, roberti.news.blog, U.S. Treasury OFAC)

name: Rosalinda González Valencia
role: El Mencho's Wife — CJNG Financial Operations ('La Jefa')
quote: Released early from a five-year money laundering sentence in early 2025 for 'good conduct,' sparking controversy. Rosalinda's González Valencia family connections to Los Cuinis made her CJNG's key financial architect. She remains free and under scrutiny.

name: Omar García Harfuch
role: Mexico Security Secretary — Led Post-Mencho Operation
quote: The man CJNG tried to kill in June 2020 ended up overseeing the operation that killed El Mencho in February 2026. As Security Secretary and former intelligence director, García Harfuch personifies Mexico's decade-long escalation against CJNG.

name: General Ricardo Trevilla Trejo
role: SEDENA Secretary (Mexican Defense Minister)
quote: Led the institutional military planning behind the Tapalpa operation. SEDENA under Trevilla has deepened intelligence-sharing with U.S. NORTHCOM and DEA while maintaining operational command authority over cartel interdiction missions on Mexican soil.

name: Claudia Sheinbaum
role: President of Mexico (since Oct 2024)
quote: Sheinbaum inherited Mexico's cartel crisis from AMLO and has navigated between maintaining her predecessor's social-investment approach and cooperating more closely with U.S. security demands following CJNG's FTO designation and the February 2026 El Mencho operation.

name: DEA Administrator
role: U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration — Lead CJNG Targeting Agency
quote: DEA has designated CJNG as one of the highest-priority Consolidated Priority Organization Targets (CPOTs). Operation Python (2020) and the sustained intelligence campaign against El Mencho represent the DEA's most intensive sustained targeting effort against a single cartel.

name: Marco Rubio
role: U.S. Secretary of State — Signed CJNG FTO Designation
quote: Secretary Rubio officially designated CJNG as a Foreign Terrorist Organization on February 20, 2025, implementing the Trump executive order and marking a major escalation in U.S. legal treatment of Mexican cartels. The designation enabled new prosecution tools against material supporters.

name: Rubén Oseguera González
role: CJNG Former Second-in-Command (Imprisoned, U.S.) — 'El Menchito'
quote: El Mencho's son and former heir apparent was extradited to the U.S. in 2020 and sentenced to life plus 30 years in March 2025, with a $6 billion forfeiture order. His imprisonment sealed CJNG's succession crisis before his father's death.

name: Érick Valencia Salazar
role: CJNG Co-founder ('El 85') — PLEADED GUILTY April 7, 2026 in U.S. Federal Court
quote: One of CJNG's original four co-founders, El 85 pleaded guilty on April 7, 2026 in U.S. District Court in Washington D.C. to conspiracy to distribute cocaine for importation into the United States — facing life in prison. Sentencing: July 31, 2026. Arrested by Mexican Army in Zapopan, Jalisco in March 2012 (triggering 12 narcobloqueos); he later broke from El Mencho to found La Nueva Plaza, a rival cartel. Extradited to U.S. February 27, 2025, part of the historic 29-figure mass extradition. His guilty plea closes the prosecution chapter on three of CJNG's four original co-founders: El Mencho (killed Feb 22, 2026), El 53 (killed 2019 by CJNG hitman), El 85 (now in U.S. custody). (Sources: U.S. DOJ, Washington Post, Washington Times)

name: José Antonio Yépez Ortiz
role: Former Santa Rosa de Lima Cartel Leader — CJNG's Rival ('El Marro')
quote: CSRL leader whose August 2020 arrest handed CJNG a decisive advantage in Guanajuato. El Marro became a social-media-savvy cartel boss who posted videos defending his gang's role in defending communities from CJNG, but ultimately could not sustain military resistance without state protection.

name: Los Viagras Leadership
role: Michoacán Rival Cartel — CJNG's Persistent Enemy in Tierra Caliente
quote: Originally a community self-defense group, Los Viagras evolved into a criminal enterprise competing with CJNG over Michoacán's avocado and lime extortion rackets. Their knowledge of local terrain gives them a 'profound advantage' in Tierra Caliente despite CJNG's superior firepower.

name: Mexican Human Rights Defenders
role: Civil Society — Documenting Cartel and State Violence
quote: Journalists and rights defenders documenting CJNG violence face severe retaliation. Mexico has remained one of the world's most dangerous countries for journalists for over a decade, with CJNG-linked threats and killings documented across Jalisco, Colima, Michoacán, and Veracruz.

name: Heraclio Guerrero Martínez
role: CJNG Regional Commander ('Tío Lako' / 'Uncle Lako') — Fourth Succession Candidate Identified by U.S. Intelligence
quote: Identified by U.S. intelligence agencies alongside O3, El Jardinero, and El Sapo as a fourth potential CJNG succession candidate. Tío Lako has managed logistics and corridor operations in northern and central Mexico for CJNG and has emerged as a figure of elevated interest as other succession candidates have been arrested or killed. His profile rose sharply following El Doble R's March 11 arrest and El Tuli's February 22 killing. No active U.S. reward announced as of March 29, 2026.

name: InSight Crime / Crisis Group Analysts
role: Independent Organized Crime Research Organizations
quote: InSight Crime and the International Crisis Group have provided the most sustained independent analysis of CJNG's structure, tactics, and succession dynamics. Their assessments consistently warn that decapitation strategies risk fragmentation that produces more violence, not less.

name: Hugo César Macías Ureña
role: CJNG Chief Financial/Logistics Officer ('El Tuli') — KILLED Feb 22, 2026
quote: Killed by Mexican special forces in El Grullo, Jalisco, in a parallel operation on Feb 22 — the same day as El Mencho. El Tuli served as CJNG's top financial/logistics architect. He had offered 20,000-peso bounties per soldier killed in retaliation. $965,000 USD and 7.2M pesos seized at time of death.

name: Hugo Gonzalo Mendoza Gaytán
role: CJNG Puerto Vallarta Plaza Boss & Chinese Precursor Coordinator ('El Sapo' / 'El 090') — ALLIED WITH EL JARDINERO vs. O3 (Apr 11, 2026)
quote: One week into the declared war on O3 and Los Cuinis, El Sapo's port logistics network at Puerto Vallarta and the Manzanillo corridor is under intensified scrutiny. The April 16 arrest of eight Manzanillo Municipal Police officers for leaking real-time C5i intelligence to CJNG via the Threema encrypted app reflects El Sapo's deep institutional penetration of port city security infrastructure — and illustrates how CJNG's two factions share legacy corrupted-police networks even as they fight each other. El Sapo's Chinese fentanyl precursor acquisition networks and Puerto Vallarta plaza logistics give the El Jardinero/El Sapo faction critical financial staying power for a protracted intra-cartel war. Status: alive; active leader of the El Jardinero/El Sapo anti-O3 armed faction. (Sources: El Imparcial, RED Michoacán, roberti.news.blog, U.S. Treasury OFAC)

name: Juan Carlos Valencia González ('O3')
role: CJNG New Leader — Confirmed Apr 6, 2026 (El País) — Grupo Élite Commander
quote: El País confirmed O3 as CJNG's new leader on April 6, 2026 — the most authoritative international media confirmation of the succession. Previously reported per anonymous cartel source (March 2026) and consistent with U.S. NCTC's pre-Mencho assessment of O3 as 'de facto second-in-command.' Born in Santa Ana, California (September 12, 1984), O3 is a U.S. citizen — creating legal complications for bilateral targeting operations. As Grupo Élite commander, he controls CJNG's greatest concentrated paramilitary firepower. Succession field has narrowed: El Doble R arrested (March 11), El Tuli killed (February 22). El Jardinero's regional power in Jalisco, Nayarit, Zacatecas, Michoacán remains a rival center. Mexican authorities have not formally confirmed. [Sources: El País, InSight Crime, IBTimes UK, U.S. State Department]

name: Omar García Harfuch
role: Mexico Security Secretary — El Pepe Arrest Statement, Mar 16
quote: The arrest of 'El Pepe' in Tlajomulco de Zúñiga represents the closure of a critical intelligence gap. This individual transported El Mencho's companion to the Tapalpa hideout — the link in the chain that ultimately led to the elimination of the most wanted criminal in Mexico's history. Mexico's security forces are systematically dismantling the entire network that protected El Mencho for decades.

name: Omar García Harfuch
role: Mexico Security Secretary — CJNG Threat Assessment, Mar 21, 2026
quote: El CJNG es una organización criminal que opera en todo el país. Despite the historic elimination of El Mencho and the sustained post-Tapalpa pressure campaign, the cartel's national operational footprint remains intact. Mexico's security forces will continue systematic dismantlement of CJNG's network — but citizens and businesses across all 27 affected states should remain vigilant.

name: FGR (Fiscalía General de la República)
role: Mexican Attorney General — El Mencho Evidence Handling Statement, Mar 2026
quote: No había condiciones para resguardar los inmuebles donde hallaron la narconómina de 'El Mencho.' The properties where El Mencho's financial ledgers and payroll records were discovered could not be adequately secured immediately after the February 22 operation. The FGR is working to preserve the evidentiary value of all materials found. Critics and opposition lawmakers have demanded a formal investigation into potential evidence tampering at the sites.
